facts.text: Upper Greenwood Lake began as a 1,500‑acre swamp called Moe before the Mirror Holding Co. dammed Long House Brook in 1932 to create the 400‑acre lake.
The Upper Greenwood Lake Property Owners Association, founded in 1933, bought the original clubhouse and surrounding parklands, turning the area into a tight‑knit community hub.
Early visitors bought 20‑by‑100‑foot lots for just $97.50 in the New York Daily Mirror, but had to clear the lakebed themselves with mule teams.
The lake’s first stumps still surface from time to time, a reminder of the unfinished clearing that caused a 2010 closure when frozen stumps broke free.
Upper Greenwood Lake sits entirely within West Milford Township in Passaic County, extending slightly into Vernon Township in Sussex County.
The community’s early social life centered around Moe’s Tavern, later renamed the Mount Laurel Inn, and other spots like Stegemann’s Tavern and the Sugar Bowl.
